name: Sycamore Hathor
description: The Sycamore tree held immense significance in ancient Egyptian culture, particularly as a sacred symbol deeply connected to the goddess Hathor. It was believed to be a manifestation of Hathor herself, providing nourishment, protection, and life-giving sustenance, especially to the deceased. The Sycamore Hathor was seen as a celestial tree, its branches reaching into the heavens, offering shade and spiritual refuge. Its milky sap was associated with Hathor's nurturing qualities and was considered a source of healing and rejuvenation. Images of Hathor emerging from the Sycamore, offering food and drink to the souls of the departed, are common in funerary art. This sacred tree was often planted near tombs and temples, symbolizing the connection between the earthly and divine realms. It represented rebirth, resurrection, and the promise of eternal life. The wood was used in crafting sacred objects and amulets, believed to carry Hathor's protective energy. Offerings were made at the base of the tree to honor Hathor and ensure her continued blessings. The Sycamore Hathor embodies the themes of motherhood, fertility, and the cyclical nature of life and death, central to the Egyptian worldview. Its presence assured the deceased of sustenance and guidance in the afterlife, provided by the benevolent goddess. The tree's enduring presence in Egyptian art and mythology underscores its profound cultural and spiritual importance, representing a powerful link between humanity and the divine.
subtitle: Sacred Tree of Nourishment and Rebirth

keyMyths.title: Hathor and the Sycamore
keyMyths.description: Hathor is often depicted emerging from the Sycamore tree, offering food and water to the deceased. This symbolizes her role as a provider and protector in the afterlife, ensuring their sustenance and well-being.

sources.author: Wilkinson, Richard H.
sources.work: Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
sources.description: Provides detailed information on Hathor and her associated symbols.
sources.text: Provides detailed information on Hathor and her associated symbols.
